verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Job, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Then Job answered and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivi frequenter talia :  consolatores onerosi omnes vos estis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁמַ֣עְתִּי כְאֵ֣לֶּה רַבּ֑וֹת מְנַחֲמֵ֖י עָמָ֣ל כֻּלְּכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: I have heard many such things: miserable comforters are ye all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid habebunt finem verba ventosa ?  aut aliquid tibi molestum est, si loquaris ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲקֵ֥ץ לְדִבְרֵי־ר֑וּחַ א֥וֹ מַה־יַּ֝מְרִֽיצְךָ֗ כִּ֣י תַֽעֲנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: Shall vain words have an end? or what emboldeneth thee that thou answerest?

verses.latin_vulgate: Poteram et ego similia vestri loqui,  atque utinam esset anima vestra pro anima mea :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֤ם ׀ אָנֹכִי֮ כָּכֶ֢ם אֲדַ֫בֵּ֥רָה ל֤וּ יֵ֪שׁ נַפְשְׁכֶ֡ם תַּ֤חַת נַפְשִׁ֗י אַחְבִּ֣ירָה עֲלֵיכֶ֣ם בְּמִלִּ֑ים וְאָנִ֥יעָה עֲ֝לֵיכֶ֗ם בְּמ֣וֹ רֹאשִֽׁי׃
verses.king_james: I also could speak as ye do: if your soul were in my soul’s stead, I could heap up words against you, and shake mine head at you.

verses.latin_vulgate: roborarem vos ore meo,  et moverem labia mea, quasi parcens vobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲאַמִּצְכֶ֥ם בְּמוֹ־פִ֑י וְנִ֖יד שְׂפָתַ֣י יַחְשֹֽׂךְ׃
verses.king_james: But I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the moving of my lips should asswage your grief.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed quid agam ? Si locutus fuero, non quiescet dolor meus,  et si tacuero, non recedet a me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִֽם־אֲ֭דַבְּרָה לֹא־יֵחָשֵׂ֣ךְ כְּאֵבִ֑י וְ֝אַחְדְּלָ֗ה מַה־מִּנִּ֥י יַהֲלֹֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Though I speak, my grief is not asswaged: and though I forbear, what am I eased?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem oppressit me dolor meus,  et in nihilum redacti sunt omnes artus mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַךְ־עַתָּ֥ה הֶלְאָ֑נִי הֲ֝שִׁמּ֗וֹתָ כׇּל־עֲדָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rugæ meæ testimonium dicunt contra me,  et suscitatur falsiloquus adversus faciem meam, contradicens mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽ֭תִּקְמְטֵנִי לְעֵ֣ד הָיָ֑ה וַיָּ֥קׇם בִּ֥י כַ֝חֲשִׁ֗י בְּפָנַ֥י יַעֲנֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: And thou hast filled me with wrinkles, which is a witness against me: and my leanness rising up in me beareth witness to my face.

verses.latin_vulgate: Collegit furorem suum in me,  et comminans mihi, infremuit contra me dentibus suis :  hostis meus terribilibus oculis me intuitus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַפּ֤וֹ טָרַ֨ף ׀ וַֽיִּשְׂטְמֵ֗נִי חָרַ֣ק עָלַ֣י בְּשִׁנָּ֑יו צָרִ֓י ׀ יִלְטֹ֖שׁ עֵינָ֣יו לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperuerunt super me ora sua,  et exprobrantes percusserunt maxillam meam :  satiati sunt pœnis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּעֲר֬וּ עָלַ֨י ׀ בְּפִיהֶ֗ם בְּ֭חֶרְפָּה הִכּ֣וּ לְחָיָ֑י יַ֝֗חַד עָלַ֥י יִתְמַלָּאֽוּן׃
verses.king_james: They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conclusit me Deus apud iniquum,  et manibus impiorum me tradidit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַסְגִּירֵ֣נִי אֵ֭ל אֶ֣ל עֲוִ֑יל וְעַל־יְדֵ֖י רְשָׁעִ֣ים יִרְטֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: God hath delivered me to the ungodly, and turned me over into the hands of the wicked.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego ille quondam opulentus, repente contritus sum :  tenuit cervicem meam, confregit me,  et posuit me sibi quasi in signum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁ֘לֵ֤ו הָיִ֨יתִי ׀ וַֽיְפַרְפְּרֵ֗נִי וְאָחַ֣ז בְּ֭עׇרְפִּי וַֽיְפַצְפְּצֵ֑נִי וַיְקִימֵ֥נִי ל֝֗וֹ לְמַטָּרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circumdedit me lanceis suis ;  convulneravit lumbos meos :  non pepercit, et effudit in terra viscera mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָ֘סֹ֤בּוּ עָלַ֨י ׀ רַבָּ֗יו יְפַלַּ֣ח כִּ֭לְיוֹתַי וְלֹ֣א יַחְמֹ֑ל יִשְׁפֹּ֥ךְ לָ֝אָ֗רֶץ מְרֵרָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: His archers compass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder, and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.

verses.latin_vulgate: Concidit me vulnere super vulnus :  irruit in me quasi gigas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִפְרְצֵ֣נִי פֶ֭רֶץ עַל־פְּנֵי־פָ֑רֶץ יָרֻ֖ץ עָלַ֣י כְּגִבּֽוֹר׃
verses.king_james: He breaketh me with breach upon breach, he runneth upon me like a giant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Saccum consui super cutem meam,  et operui cinere carnem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שַׂ֣ק תָּ֭פַרְתִּי עֲלֵ֣י גִלְדִּ֑י וְעֹלַ֖לְתִּי בֶעָפָ֣ר קַרְנִֽי׃
verses.king_james: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and defiled my horn in the dust.

verses.latin_vulgate: Facies mea intumuit a fletu,  et palpebræ meæ caligaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּנַ֣י (חמרמרה) [חֳ֭מַרְמְרוּ] מִנִּי־בֶ֑כִי וְעַ֖ל עַפְעַפַּ֣י צַלְמָֽוֶת׃
verses.king_james: My face is foul with weeping, and on my eyelids is the shadow of death;

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc passus sum absque iniquitate manus meæ,  cum haberem mundas ad Deum preces.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַ֭ל לֹא־חָמָ֣ס בְּכַפָּ֑י וּֽתְפִלָּתִ֥י זַכָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Not for any injustice in mine hands: also my prayer is pure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Terra, ne operias sanguinem meum,  neque inveniat in te locum latendi clamor meus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֭רֶץ אַל־תְּכַסִּ֣י דָמִ֑י וְֽאַל־יְהִ֥י מָ֝ק֗וֹם לְזַעֲקָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: O earth, cover not thou my blood, and let my cry have no place.

verses.latin_vulgate: ecce enim in cælo testis meus,  et conscius meus in excelsis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־עַ֭תָּה הִנֵּה־בַשָּׁמַ֣יִם עֵדִ֑י וְ֝שָׂהֲדִ֗י בַּמְּרֹמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Also now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and my record is on high.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verbosi amici mei :  ad Deum stillat oculus meus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְלִיצַ֥י רֵעָ֑י אֶל־אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ דָּלְפָ֥ה עֵינִֽי׃
verses.king_james: My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God.

verses.latin_vulgate: atque utinam sic judicaretur vir cum Deo,  quomodo judicatur filius hominis cum collega suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיוֹכַ֣ח לְגֶ֣בֶר עִם־אֱל֑וֹהַּ וּֽבֶן־אָדָ֥ם לְרֵעֵֽהוּ׃
verses.king_james: O that one might plead for a man with God, as a man pleadeth for his neighbour!

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce enim breves anni transeunt,  et semitam per quam non revertar ambulo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־שְׁנ֣וֹת מִסְפָּ֣ר יֶאֱתָ֑יוּ וְאֹ֖רַח לֹא־אָשׁ֣וּב אֶהֱלֹֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: When a few years are come, then I shall go the way whence I shall not return.
